Getting Started:
Install qora_flutter, create a single QoraClient with QoraClientConfig(defaultOptions: QoraOptions(...), reconnectStrategy, maxCacheSize, debugMode).
Mount QoraScope(client: ...) at app root to expose the client through context.qora, QoraScope.of(context), and QoraScope.maybeOf(context).
Attach FlutterLifecycleManager and FlutterConnectivityManager in QoraScope for resume-triggered and reconnect-triggered invalidation/refetch.
Validate bootstrap with a first QoraBuilder or watchQuery using normalized keys (List or QoraKey).

Core Concepts:
State model is QoraState<T> with Initial, Loading(previousData), Success(data, updatedAt), Failure(error, previousData).
SWR lifecycle is controlled by staleTime: serve cached state immediately, then revalidate in background through fetchQuery or watchQuery.
Cache lifecycle is controlled by cacheTime, LRU pressure (maxCacheSize), and explicit invalidation (invalidate, invalidateWhere, removeQuery, clear).
Key consistency uses normalizeKey, QoraKey, and deep structural equality for cache hits, deduplication, and targeted invalidation.

Guides:
Offline behavior is defined by NetworkMode (online, always, offlineFirst) and runtime FetchStatus (fetching, paused, idle) via watchFetchStatus and builder callbacks.
Retry policy uses retryCount, retryDelay, retryDelayCalculator with exponential backoff, plus global error normalization via errorMapper.
Request deduplication merges concurrent same-key fetches into one in-flight Future, then fan-outs the result to all waiters/subscribers.
Cooperative cancellation uses CancelToken in fetchQuery, watchQuery, prefetch, and surfaces QoraCancelException on abort.

Flutter Integration:
QoraBuilder<T> binds queryKey, fetcher, and QoraOptions, rendering (BuildContext, QoraState<T>, FetchStatus) with automatic re-fetch on invalidation.
QoraMutationBuilder<TData, TVariables, TContext> manages mutation lifecycle and exposes mutate with hooks (onMutate, onSuccess, onError, onSettled).
InfiniteQueryBuilder<TData, TPageParam> wraps InfiniteQueryObserver and exposes InfiniteQueryController.fetchNextPage, fetchPreviousPage, refetch.
Hooks API (useQuery, useMutation, useInfiniteQuery, useQueryClient, useIsFetching, useIsMutating) provides composable server-state bindings without manual widget state.

API Reference:
QoraClient core methods: fetchQuery, watchQuery, prefetch, getQueryData, getQueryState, setQueryData, restoreQueryData, invalidate, invalidateWhere, debugInfo.
Global config is QoraClientConfig: defaultOptions, refetchOnMount, reconnectStrategy, errorMapper, onCacheEvict, maxCacheSize, debugMode.
Per-query config is QoraOptions: staleTime, cacheTime, enabled, dependsOn, retryCount, refetchInterval, networkMode.
Persistence API is PersistQoraClient with registerSerializer, hydrate, persistQuery, evictFromStorage, clearStorage, backed by StorageAdapter.

DevTools:
Two tooling surfaces: in-app overlay (QoraInspector, OverlayTracker) and IDE extension (VmTracker in Flutter DevTools).
Observability pipeline is driven by QoraTracker with NoOpTracker fallback in release to remove tracking emissions and runtime overhead.
Real-time inspection covers queries, mutations, event timeline, retry metadata, rollback context, plus command actions like Refetch and Invalidate.
IDE extension adds network activity metrics, per-key performance stats, dependency graphing, and chunked payload retrieval for large cache objects.

Integrations:
With Provider, BLoC, and Riverpod: keep UI state/DI where it is, move server-state read/write orchestration to QoraClient, QoraBuilder, and mutation APIs.
BLoC integration pattern: inject QoraClient into write-oriented blocs and call invalidate after successful domain mutations.
Riverpod integration pattern: run ProviderScope with QoraScope, replace FutureProvider read paths with useQuery or QoraBuilder where SWR/previousData is required.
qora_hooks supplies typed handles (MutationHandle, InfiniteQueryHandle) while sharing the same cache graph as widget builders.

Examples:
CRUD examples use fetchQuery for reads, MutationController or QoraMutationBuilder for writes, then setQueryData/restoreQueryData plus invalidateWhere for consistency.
Infinite scroll examples use InfiniteQueryObserver with InfiniteQueryOptions(getNextPageParam, maxPages) and flatten to render page-accumulated lists.
Offline-first examples combine PersistQoraClient, hydrate, and QoraSerializer<T> registration to warm cache before first frame.
Reconnect sync examples use OfflineMutationQueue, ReconnectStrategy, and NetworkMode.offlineFirst to replay queued writes and converge cache with server state.
